chapter_title.en: HADEETH ABOUT THE ONE WHO IS BORN IN AL-ISLAM

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5 / 373)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5 / 373)

parts.text: عَمِّهِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عِيسَى بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ

text: أَخَذَ أَبِي بِيَدِي ثُمَّ قَالَ يَا بُنَيَّ إِنَّ أَبِي مُحَمَّدَ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ (عليه السلام) أَخَذَ بِيَدِي كَمَا أَخَذْتُ بِيَدِكَ وَ قَالَ إِنَّ أَبِي عَلِيَّ بْنَ الْحُسَيْنِ (عليه السلام) أَخَذَ بِيَدِي وَ قَالَ يَا بُنَيَّ افْعَلِ الْخَيْرَ إِلَى كُلِّ مَنْ طَلَبَهُ مِنْكَ فَإِنْ كَانَ مِنْ أَهْلِهِ فَقَدْ أَصَبْتَ مَوْضِعَهُ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ مِنْ أَهْلِهِ كُنْتَ أَنْتَ مِنْ أَهْلِهِ وَ إِنْ شَتَمَكَ رَجُلٌ عَنْ يَمِينِكَ ثُمَّ تَحَوَّلَ إِلَى يَسَارِكَ فَاعْتَذَرَ إِلَيْكَ فَاقْبَلْ عُذْرَهُ.

en.hubeali: H 14589 – Muhammad Bin Abu Abdullah, from Musa Bin Umraan, from his uncle Al-Husayn Bin Isa Bin Abdullah, from Ali Bin Ja’far, from his brother Abu Al-Hassan Al-Musaasws having said:

‘Myasws father grabbed myasws hand then said: ‘O myasws sonasws, myasws fatherasws Muhammad Bin Aliasws grabbed myasws hand just as Iasws have grabbed yourasws hand, and said: ‘Myasws fatherasws Aliasws Bin Al-Husaynasws grabbed myasws hand and said: ‘Myasws sonasws, do good to everyone who seeks it from youasws, so if he was deserving of it then it has found its place, and if he was not deserving of it, then youasws were deserving of it, and if a man insults you from yourasws right, then turn to yourasws left. So if he offers his excuse for it, accept his excuse’.
en.sarwar: 141. Muhammad Bin Abu Abdullah, from Musa Bin Umraan, from his uncle Al-Husayn Bin Isa Bin Abdullah, from Ali Bin Ja’far, from his brother Abu Al-Hassan Al-Musa (a.s.) having said: ‘My (a.s.) father grabbed my (a.s.) hand then said: ‘O my (a.s.) son (a.s.), my (a.s.) father (a.s.) Muhammad Bin Ali (a.s.) grabbed my (a.s.) hand just as I (a.s.) have grabbed your (a.s.) hand, and said: ‘My (a.s.) father (a.s.) Ali (a.s.) Bin Al-Husayn (a.s.) grabbed my (a.s.) hand and said: ‘My (a.s.) son (a.s.), do good to everyone who seeks it from you (a.s.), so if he was deserving of it then it has found its place, and if he was not deserving of it, then you (a.s.) were deserving of it, and if a man insults you from your (a.s.) right, then turn to your (a.s.) left. So if he offers his excuse for it, accept his excuse’.
